title: combretum tetrandrum subsp. tetrandrum
intro: Combretum tetrandrum subsp. tetrandrum, also known as 'Mangrove Combretum', is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The bark is rich in tannins, which provide anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, while the leaves have been used to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for use in herbal remedies, and has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
